$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 15 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The circus spent the season weeping at someone else's story, and the king let it. Senna did not join the last climb; he remained the summit it had to reach, unhurried, immaculate, the MADONNA 456 and the PALM 190 V10 setting the time the pilgrimage spent every afternoon failing to reach. Asselin kept the door with the usual gloved calm. The yellow-and-red garage watched the thousand small names roll past its pit bay and was seen, once, to come out and look, which at Madonna counts as applause. The crown does not weep. It measures.
team.madonna.objective: Remain the summit the last climb is measured against, and be moved by none of it.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The Madonna garage comes out to look at the hand-lettered car, once, which counts as applause.
team.madonna.carryForward: The Jubilee will parade the king among the crowned names; he intends to arrive as the present tense.

team.firenze.summary: The ice pressed its decade-long argument while the paddock watched the pilgrimage. Elssler's FIRENZE 500 remained the coldest instrument in the compressed field, the FIRENZE 99 V12's aristocratic manners intact, the alpine arithmetic banking what the noise spilled. Germi gave Italy its temple opera and found the afternoon belonged, for once, to someone else's story: the handshake up the pit lane. The wine-dark house's year was the connoisseur's kind, excellent, quiet, and third in everyone's telling. Second to the crown, and beginning to suspect the crown preferred it that way.
team.firenze.objective: Bank the alpine argument while the circus watches the pilgrimage.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Italy belongs to someone else's story, and the wine-dark house takes note, and notes.
team.firenze.carryForward: The Jubilee will honour the house's bloodline; the ice wants it to honour the present too.

team.millions.summary: The gold garage spent the pilgrimage year in the unfamiliar position of being out-sentimentalized. While the backstreet car carried a thousand small names past every motorhome, the MILLIONS 189 ran its expensive, excellent campaign, Alberti banking patrician arithmetic, Jones roaring at the appropriate volumes, the DICK MD V10 polished and faithful. The chrome garage's accountants tried, for one memo, to compute the cost-per-name on the #17's flanks, and gave up. The season's lesson reached even the gold: some currencies are not for sale. The garage's response was pure Millions: it resolved to buy whatever it was anyway.
team.millions.objective: Run the expensive campaign, and work out what the thousand names are worth.
team.millions.definingEvent: The accountants abandon the cost-per-name memo after one meeting.
team.millions.carryForward: The Jubilee is a buyer's market in heritage, and the gold intends to be photographed paying respects.

team.bestowal.summary: The cathedral pressed its crown argument with German thoroughness and found the season's heart elsewhere. Blume's metronomic correctness and Picos's warm conversion kept the BESTOWAL 167 in the front rows the compressed field allowed, the VAPOR DN's delivery the fast circuits' standing reference, but the marble house knew the difference between a campaign and a story, and this was someone else's story. The benefactor, to his credit, recognized the thousand small names for what they were: expectation, the house currency, carried by the poor. The cathedral's mood was respect, audited and approved.
team.bestowal.objective: Press the crown argument, and audit the season's romance with patrician honesty.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The benefactor recognizes the thousand names as expectation carried by the poor, and approves.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The Jubilee will ask every house what it stands for; the cathedral's answer will be invoice and altar both.

team.blanche.summary: The white car pressed every Sunday, and no front row was safe. Herbin's spotless craft, honed into the grid's reference, kept the BLANCHE 582 in the establishment's mirrors all season, the DELTA 103 V10's thrift and thunder the midfield's standing argument, while the pilgrimage took the headlines. White added brave afternoons and unquenchable grin. The whitecoats preferred it this way: the champagne in the garage stayed cold, the laps stayed cleaner than anyone's, and the aristocrats kept glancing at the white car in their mirrors with the particular unease of men who have stopped being able to explain it.
team.blanche.objective: Press the aristocrats every Sunday until the white car is the standing explanation.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The white car becomes the unease the aristocrats cannot explain, weekly.
team.blanche.carryForward: The Jubilee bunting will include white, and the champagne's moment is getting closer.

team.tyrant.summary: The blades were measured, and the measuring went well. Hamano's crossover discipline kept the TYRANT 548 in the thick of the golden age's compressed front, the LIZZIE 24 V8 finishing everything with boot-camp regularity, and Japan got its annual discipline lesson before the home crowd. Gould preached at Canada, the island sermon now a fixture of the calendar. The gunmetal garage watched the backstreet pilgrimage with professional approval: one uncompromised car, aimed at the crown, was a creed the boot camp had always understood. The crew's own ledger was quieter: steady, menacing, and saving its charge.
team.tyrant.objective: Hold the compressed front, and save one charge for when the golden age blinks.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: The boot camp salutes the one-car gamble as a creed it recognizes.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The Jubilee will be soft-hearted; the boot camp intends to be the exception.

team.losel.summary: The model said Losel keeps climbing, and the model, as usual, was right. Pacheco's cold arithmetic kept the LOSEL 125 climbing through the golden age's compressed midfield, the VAPOR DNPQ V8's curves all holding, Dehehe ticking in support, while the data door read the season's real experiment with professional envy: the backstreet garage's one-car gamble was, the slide rules admitted, a beautiful proof. Desperation designs beautifully; Losel had the equation for it within a fortnight. The garage's own campaign was its usual solved proof, banked quietly while the circus wept at someone else's romance.
team.losel.objective: Keep climbing per the model, and solve the pilgrimage as a proof.
team.losel.definingEvent: The slide rules derive the one-car gamble in a fortnight, and file it under beautiful.
team.losel.carryForward: The Jubilee's numbers will be soft; the data door's will not, and the gap is the plan.

team.may.summary: Spring kept its teeth. Turner and the MAY 555 spent the pilgrimage year mugging the distracted in the compressed midfield, the LORRY 32 V8 finishing with the loyalty of a family dog, and the census recorded another green afternoon the big board had not priced. The crew watched the thousand small names go past, understood immediately, and passed a helmet round the garage for the backstreet fund, leaf-green stamps and all. The youngest crew on the grid had learned the golden age's best lesson: hope with teeth is a strategy, and hope with names on the car is a movement.
team.may.objective: Mug the distracted, and pass the helmet for the movement.
team.may.definingEvent: The garage passes a helmet round for the backstreet fund, leaf-green stamps and all.
team.may.carryForward: The Jubilee will celebrate movements; May intends to be the youngest one honoured.

team.bullets.summary: The last climb. The garage sold everything not bolted to the #17, spares, transporter, the trophies' display cases, and where sponsors should sit the hand-built BULLETS 560 carried a thousand small names instead, the people the establishment never sees, paying by whip-round. Ceara spent the bravest campaign of his life at the front, Brazil a pilgrimage of flags stitched with the 17, Belgium the wet master's shrine, and at Italy the decade's oldest war ended in one handshake. The summit, gold and serene, stayed exactly where it was. The climb was the point.
team.bullets.objective: Stake the garage's entire existence on one uncompromised assault at the crown.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The hand-lettered car rolls out at San Marino, and the grandstand reads the names one by one.
team.bullets.carryForward: The fortune is spent; whatever the Jubilee brings, the garage now races on borrowed everything.

team.dardan.summary: The last page was written at Italy, with an open hand. Bellini walked the length of the pit lane in front of the tifosi and publicly shook Gilberto Ceara's hand, and the decade-long chassis feud ended, loudly and once. The oxblood garage wept, cheered, and discovered that peace, properly struck, pays better than war: the DARDAN 700 ran freed all season, Alfven's ice and the old spear's new grace banking the house's best years. The VAPOR DNPQ V8 sang. The clan that never kneels had learned a harder thing: how to let go standing up.
team.dardan.objective: End the feud the way the best stories end: loudly, once, and in front of the tifosi.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The Italy handshake: Bellini walks the pit lane and ends a decade of chassis war in one gesture.
team.dardan.carryForward: The oxblood house is at peace for the first time in memory, and peace is fast.

team.linden.summary: The elder watched the pilgrimage with the smile of a man who had seen every kind of climb. Moreau's own season was the usual miracle of ordinariness, the LINDEN LN198 finishing everything, and the longest tenure the championship has ever kept deepened another year. The LORRY 32 V8 seasoned past all argument. The green garage passed the hat for the backstreet fund, and the old philosopher was heard to say the thousand names were the season's true engineering. The watch kept being set. The root kept growing. The golden age had a conscience, and it was green.
team.linden.objective: Deepen the tenure, pass the hat, and name the season's true engineering.
team.linden.definingEvent: Moreau calls the thousand names the season's true engineering, and the paddock quotes him.
team.linden.carryForward: The Jubilee will celebrate twenty-five seasons; the elder has lived all of them in green.

team.minarae.summary: The cheque was cashed. Nono's full arrival turned the forge into the ladder's loudest address: the boy who ate hairpins for breakfast started converting them, the MINARAE 594 and the SEGA SG1000 V8 finally in machinery equal to the talent, and the #21's name moved from the Friday circle to the Sunday argument. Miller stood on the pit wall like the proudest measuring stick in racing, watched his protege outgrow him, and called it the best result of his career. The paddock had stopped laughing altogether. The forge had produced its flame, and the golden age had an alarm clock.
team.minarae.objective: Cash the cheque the talent always promised, and turn the forge into the ladder's forge.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono's breakout takes hold, and Miller calls it the best result of his career.
team.minarae.carryForward: The flame is lit; the Jubilee will be the first season the forge runs as an established address.

team.rigel.summary: The old stars watched the backstreet fire and remembered their own. Rigel's jewel-era campaign was its best of the modern age, the RIGEL 3000 finishing with the regularity the lifers had spent a decade restoring toward, Cotman banking finishes, Chardin holding doors, the LORRY 32 V8 singing its bright-century song. The garage passed the hat for the backstreet fund with grease-stained notes and a card that read: from one old light to another. The trophy shelf caught the work-lamps a little longer each evening. The starlight, the lifers agreed, was no longer a direction. It was a destination again.
team.rigel.objective: Bank the best modern campaign, and salute the backstreet fire from one old light.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The card with the hat: from one old light to another.
team.rigel.carryForward: The Jubilee will honour old lights; the museum intends to be counted among them.

team.comet.summary: The black car answered the golden age early: Tornio put the COMET 323 onto a front row in the spring, silently, as is the custom, and the whole garage pretended it happened every week. The LIZZIE 24 V8 finished nearly everything, and the romantics spent the season one step closer to the sky than the year before. The pilgrimage suited the comet theology: a thousand small names funding one blaze was their own story with the numbers filled in. The pan stayed cold. The faith stayed warm. The old reel got eleven new laps beside it, and did not mind sharing.
team.comet.objective: Put the black car on one more front row, and keep the faith warm.
team.comet.definingEvent: A silent spring front row, pretended about as routine, believed in as scripture.
team.comet.carryForward: The Jubilee loves a blaze story; the comet-chasers own the original.

team.orchis.summary: The harvest came in, both of them. The ORCHIS 056's survival doctrine and the MISFIRE 50 V8's fifth clean season made the little glasshouse the midfield's coolest head, Tegner banking grey-afternoon arithmetic with a professor's patience while the circus swooned over the pilgrimage. The gardener-principal, a romantic of a different climate, sent a cutting to the backstreet fund with a note: blooms understand climbs. The frost gambler's ledger recorded the house's best year, again. The flower had learned to live everywhere, and the golden age, with all its noise, had not once made it wilt.
team.orchis.objective: Harvest the doctrine, and send the climb a cutting.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The cutting with the note: blooms understand climbs.
team.orchis.carryForward: The Jubilee will celebrate survivors; the flower has become one without noticing.

team.zeroforce.summary: The points day came within one lap of arriving, and the whole pit lane aged a year waiting. Klinger brought the ZEROFORCE 231 home one position short of the chalk the garage dreams in, and twenty-three garages put the toast back in the fridge without being asked to. The floor's season was otherwise its best ever: finishes as routine, the #32 a fixture rather than a miracle. The gallows humour had to be retrained entirely. The end of the road, the crew agreed over the slightly better coffee, now had a view and a reservation.
team.zeroforce.objective: Finish everything, and take the points day to its final lap.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: One lap short: the toast goes back in the fridge in twenty-three garages.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The reservation stands, and the Jubilee would be the perfect room for the toast.

team.joke.summary: While the circus wept at the pilgrimage, the carnival picked its pockets, which is the highest tribute the Joke garage pays. Dufay's fake-strategy theatre had its finest season in the compressed field, the JOKE 777 appearing in lead groups it had no business visiting and the POND V8 running like a pickpocket's fingers. The ledger held high in LEVEL B. The #16's contribution to the backstreet fund was a single coin, a speech, and a note: this is how you run a con, sincerely, professionals. The grandstands howled. The golden age, everyone agreed, would be unbearable without him.
team.joke.objective: Pick the pilgrimage's pockets, and pay the climb the tribute of a proper con.
team.joke.definingEvent: The single coin, the speech, and the note: sincerely, professionals.
team.joke.carryForward: The Jubilee is the biggest stage the carnival has ever been handed, and it is planning something.

team.lares.summary: The hearth's faith, already proven, simply kept being right. Arai brought the LARES 92 home through the pilgrimage year with the usual courteous reliability, the RAM V12 finishing the season entire, and the mantel filled at the steady rate the house calls abundance. The garage's contribution to the backstreet fund was a supper invitation, standing, and a hearth-red stamp the size of a door. The paddock loved the little house for it. The golden age got loud and beautiful and expensive, and the lamp stayed lit, the whole sermon, preached every Sunday without a word.
team.lares.objective: Keep the lamp lit, and answer the climb with a standing invitation to supper.
team.lares.definingEvent: The hearth-red stamp the size of a door joins the backstreet fund.
team.lares.carryForward: The Jubilee will honour the paddock's heart; the hearth has been keeping it warm all along.

team.feet.summary: The owed season was collected, in sweat. Rampal and the FEET 13 spent the pilgrimage year fighting in the compressed pack with all three tools, the YOUGEN V10 answering every ask, and Old Boots came home in the points the garage now called wages. The grafters' contribution to the backstreet fund was a weekend's labour, volunteered, and a handshake that meant more than the money. The pit lane's caps stayed tipped. The golden age had proved what the working man's team always insisted: machinery respects the hands that build it, and the hands were owed.
team.feet.objective: Collect the owed season, and pay the climb in labour rather than coin.
team.feet.definingEvent: A weekend's volunteered labour joins the backstreet fund, with a handshake.
team.feet.carryForward: The Jubilee will honour what the sport is for; the grafters have been the answer all along.

team.serga.summary: The parable was retold at national scale, and the original watched with greasy hands and wet eyes. The backstreet car carrying a thousand small names was the grey car's whole creed inflated into a movement, and Sambena, rescued by the paddock once, recognized every rivet of it. His own season was the usual honest account, the SERGA 1000's SC3000 F12 finishing stubbornly, the welding never done. At Brazil the mountain man stood in the crowd and waved a small grey flag. Nobody recognized him. He said afterwards that was the best part.
team.serga.objective: Watch the creed become a movement, and wave a small grey flag in the crowd.
team.serga.definingEvent: The small grey flag at Brazil, unrecognized, and called the best part.
team.serga.carryForward: The Jubilee will tell the parable back to its author; he will pretend not to hear.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs had the pilgrimage year circled from the moment the names went on the car, and it did not disappoint. Cool watched the whole beautiful, roaring season from the best seats in the paddock, Delvaux chasing the light with the COOL 05's CORSE V8 humming approval, and declared the golden age an unqualified success, mostly because of other people. The garage's festivals came at their usual intervals, each celebrated until the light failed. Its contribution to the backstreet fund was a deckchair, auctioned, with a plaque: for the climb. The light went gold. The glass was raised.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the pilgrimage from the deckchairs, and auction one for the climb.
team.cool.definingEvent: The auctioned deckchair, with a plaque: for the climb.
team.cool.carryForward: The Jubilee will be the greatest show the deckchairs have ever faced, and they are ready.

team.moon.summary: The waxing continued, and the pilgrimage year gave the far side its favourite argument yet: names on a car could fund a climb, and Moon had been keeping its lights on for a decade. The MOON 292's RAM V12 completed another full orbit of finishes, Yepes waving and grinning from closer than ever, and the chalk gap shrank by its annual degree. The low voices noted that the thousand names had started as a whip-round, the whip-round as a small grey car's rescue: everything great, the telescope concluded, starts small and far away. The garage knew a little about that.
team.moon.objective: Shrink the gap another degree, and file the pilgrimage under proof.
team.moon.definingEvent: The telescope's conclusion: everything great starts small and far away.
team.moon.carryForward: The Jubilee will celebrate beginnings; Moon's is still happening.

team.iris.summary: The fire burned on beneath the season the paddock loved, and refused to mind. Salgado's second-crown obsession kept the IRIS 717 in the compressed field's front rows, the PRISM 90 V10 singing at the razor's edge, the white gloves immaculate through sixteen rounds of someone else's romance. The violet house understood the thousand names better than most: the flame had his own crowd at Portugal, and the vow still had its clock. The cathedral's campaign was total, elegant and loud, and the second crown stayed one reach ahead. The fire had not cooled; the clock had not stopped.
team.iris.objective: Burn beneath the romance, and keep the vow's clock audible over it.
team.iris.definingEvent: The flame keeps his own crowd at Portugal while the paddock weeps at someone else's.
team.iris.carryForward: The Jubilee will parade the fire among the crowned; he intends to parade as a contender.

team.azalea.summary: The cold arithmetic pressed the aristocrats every Sunday, and no front row was safe. Larssen's consistency made the AZALEA 808 the season's quietest menace, the #34 banking positions while the pilgrimage took the headlines, the BLOOM 88 V8 flawless, the rooftop lung collecting Mexico on schedule. The glasshouse watched the sister house's fire burn beneath the romance and said nothing, which said everything. The frost's crown argument was no longer an argument anyone made politely; it was arithmetic on the big board. The bloom held. The decade's cultivation was now, simply, the coldest fact in the golden age.
team.azalea.objective: Press the aristocrats every Sunday, and make the frost a big-board fact.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The #34's arithmetic stops being polite and starts being a fact.
team.azalea.carryForward: The Jubilee's tightest field will have the frost at its coldest, exactly as planted.
